sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Mr. James, is a 63-year-old male with a history of pain in his left shoulder and elbow, which began 5 months ago after a fall on icy stairs.
He reports constant pain, which worsens with lifting and has prevented him from continuing his regular exercise routine at the gym.
He denies numbness or tingling in his arm.
He has been trying to manage the pain with ice but has not taken any medication.
Past medical history:
The patient has a past medical history of a fall 5 months ago and a recent retirement from an office job.
Review of systems:
The patient denies any symptoms in other organs that are relevant to his initial presentation.
Current medications:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam:
The left shoulder and elbow were tender to palpation, with a positive Neer's test and a positive kiss.
There was no warmth, erythema, or deformity.
The patient had normal proximal and distal upper extremity strength, intact median, radial, and ulnar sensation, and abduction to 90 degrees.
The empty can test was normal.
Test Results:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is experiencing impingement syndrome of the left shoulder.
Problem list: Impingement syndrome of left shoulder; Active.
Plan:
I prescribed Mr. James to continue icing his shoulder and rest as much as possible.
I ordered a referral for physical therapy with Dr. Martha Rivera, 2 days a week.
I will have the physical therapy office contact Mr. James within the next day or two to schedule an appointment.
I will also have Mr. James follow up with me if his symptoms worsen or do not improve with therapy.
Follow-up:
I will schedule a follow-up appointment with Mr. James in 2-3 weeks to assess the effectiveness of the physical therapy and to discuss further management if necessary.
